bibliographic_info.title: Lightweight seamless lane-level positioning with the integration of BDS-3 PPP-B2b and VINS: a case study using smartphone
bibliographic_info.authors: Rui Shang, Chengfa Gao, Xiaolin Meng, Zhenhao Xu, Qi Liu, Qianqian Chen, Xiangdong An

grounding_quotes: <!-- PAGE: 1 -->  GPS Solutions (2024) 28:185 https://doi.org/10.1007/s10291-024-01730-7 ORIGINAL ARTICLE Lightweight seamless lane‑level positioning with the integration of BDS‑3 PPP‑B2b and VINS: a case study using smartphone Rui Shang1 · Chengfa Gao1 · Xiaolin Meng2 · Zhenhao Xu1 · Qi Liu1 · Qianqian Chen3 · Xiangdong An4 Received: 11 April 2024 / Accepted: 16 August 2024 / Published online: 23

Therefore, this paper proposes a lightweight seamless lane-level positioning method for smartphones with the integration of PPP and VINS based on BeiDou navigation satellite system (BDS-3) PPP-B2b services.
When structures like bridges or highway toll booths are recognized, the factor graph algorithm combines PPP-B2b/VINS to achieve positioning.
